question.text: What stamp duty obligation applies in case of transfer of securities pursuant to invocation of pledge?

answer.text: Stamp duty shall be collected from the pledgee on the market value of the securities
Stamp duty shall be collected from the pledgor on the face value of the securities
No stamp duty applies on pledge invocation
Stamp duty is equally shared between pledgor and pledgee

explanation.text: **The Logic:** As per Rule 5(7) of the Indian Stamp Rules, 2019, in case of transfer of securities pursuant to invocation of pledge, duty shall be collected from the pledgee on the market value of the securities. **The Trap:** One might assume the pledgor pays since they defaulted, but it's the pledgee who pays on market value. **Cross-Exam Context:** Stamp duty on various depository transactions is covered in NISM Series VI. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Factor in stamp duty costs when calculating recovery from pledge invocation — the pledgee bears this cost on market value.
